text: Best chicken and waffles on Livernois, hands down. The wings come out hot with that crispy skin and the waffle is light, not greasy. Brought Denise here after early service and she cleaned her plate. Worth the wait on a Sunday.

Line wraps out the door but it moves. Got the smothered chicken and the grits were done right, not that instant stuff. Knocked off a star because parking on the Avenue is a headache. Food makes up for it.

Vicki's rib tips are a Detroit institution and they have not slipped one bit. Smoke ring on that BBQ is real, sauce has the right tang, and the greens taste like somebody's grandmother made them. Get the cornbread too.

Stopped on McNichols for a slab to go. BBQ was fall-off-the-bone and the candied yams were sweet without being syrupy. Counter service was a little slow at lunch but the man working was friendly about it.

Norma G's jerk chicken has a slow burn that builds and the oxtail is rich and tender. Took Denise here for our anniversary and the staff treated us like family. Best Caribbean food on the east side, no contest.

Curry goat was on point and the rice and peas had real coconut flavor. Service ran a touch behind because they were slammed, but the food was worth waiting on. Plantains could have been a little sweeter.

A Coney downtown is a Detroit rite of passage. Chili dog with onions and mustard, loose burger on the side, done in five minutes. Service is quick and no-nonsense. Been coming here since I was a young man on the line.

Coney is solid and open late which counts for a lot. The chili is good but the fries were limp the night I went. Still, you cannot beat it after a Tigers game. Wait staff hustled even with a packed house.

The eternal debate between Lafayette and American, and I lean Lafayette by a hair. Chili is meatier here. Place is cash-only and tight on space but the Coneys come out fast and hot. Detroit through and through.

Southern plate done right. The fried catfish was flaky, the collard greens had smoked turkey in them, and the mac and cheese was baked, not the boxed kind. Service was warm and they kept my sweet tea full.

Cajun spot in Greektown with real flavor. The blackened catfish and the crawfish etouffee both hit. Bananas Foster done tableside is a nice touch. A little pricey and loud on a Saturday but the service kept up.

Beans and Cornbread is upscale soul food but it never forgets its roots. The smothered pork chops melt and the greens are seasoned to perfection. Took the whole family after church and every plate came back clean.

Gospel brunch with a live choir and shrimp and grits that do not quit. The atmosphere lifts your spirit and the food matches it. Reservation is a must. Knocked off a star because the buffet line backed up around eleven.

Bert's in Eastern Market on a Saturday is the move. Got the smothered chicken and greens after walking the sheds. Live music kicked in and the whole room was moving. Service was friendly even with the crowd.

Soul food and live jazz under one roof. The catfish dinner came with hush puppies and a side of greens that had real pot likker. Detroit history in this room. The staff made my out-of-town cousin feel right at home.

Hidden gem on Woodward. The oxtails were rich and the candied yams were the best I have had outside my own kitchen. Cornbread came hot with honey butter. Small spot, big heart, and the owner thanked us on the way out.

Smokey G's brisket is smoked low and slow the way it ought to be. Pulled pork too. I bring my own appetite and leave with leftovers. The BBQ sauce on the side lets the smoke do the talking. Counter folks are good people.

Seafood in Greektown that delivers. The fried catfish platter was generous and the gumbo had a deep roux. Service was attentive and they split the check without a fuss. Get there early on weekends or expect a wait.

Wing King knows wings. Got the lemon pepper and the honey gold, both crispy and meaty. Add a side of greens and you have a full meal. Carryout only and the wait can run long on a Friday but the service is friendly.

Mama Dean's is comfort food that tastes like home. The smothered turkey wings over rice and the cabbage with smoked turkey took me back. Portions are huge and the prices are fair. The whole staff calls you baby and means it.

Catfish Corner is exactly what the name promises. Fresh catfish fried golden, light cornmeal breading, and a vinegar hot sauce that wakes you up. Hush puppies and a side of greens round it out. Service was quick and kind.
Best chicken and waffles on the Avenue. Line moves fast, wings hot and crispy. Denise cleaned her plate.
Solid soul food spot. Greens taste homemade. Parking's tight but worth it.
Rib tips fall off the bone. Sauce has the right vinegar tang. Bring cash for the counter.
Cornbread is skillet-baked right. Family stayed for seconds and thirds.
Catfish fried perfect, breading not too heavy. Hush puppies could be crispier.
Sweet potato pie almost as good as Denise makes. That's saying something.
Sunday brunch after service done right. Grits creamy, biscuits warm.
Coney dog Detroit style. Chili had flavor. Old school counter service.
BBQ brisket was tender and had a proper smoke ring. Not everybody gets that.
Wait was long on a Sunday but I get it. Food came out consistent.
Sweet tea comes in a real glass jar. Perfect on a hot day.
Best mac and cheese on the east side. Not too much cheese, done right.
Comforted us after a long shift. Kind counter staff, real Detroit hospitality.
Live gospel Sunday brunch. Church, food, and family in one spot.
Fried chicken crispy and juicy. Coleslaw a little too sweet for me.
The Block is a Detroit institution. Never a bad plate off that kitchen.
Ordered the smothered chicken. Real gravy, not the packet kind.
Denise loves the sweet potato dessert. We split a slice.
Old school. My mother used to eat here. Still tastes the same.
Portions could feed two. Perfect for a working lunch break.
Fish fry Friday is the move. Corner is always packed for a reason.
Best comfort food after a rough week at the plant. Absolutely worth it.
Ambience feels like family Sunday. Music low, food hot, service friendly.
Waffle came out light but not crispy enough. Chicken made up for it.
Southern breakfast done right. Grits, biscuits, salmon patty.
That candied yam side is dangerous. Sweet, not too syrupy.
Buttered rolls stole the show. Big appetite required.
Best kept secret in the neighborhood. Locals only, that's a good sign.
Church potluck couldn't compete with this greens plate. And that's high praise.
Brought Ruth here for her birthday, she said the peach cobbler took her back.
Catfish nuggets crispy, and the tartar sauce is homemade.
The kind of place I bring Darnell when we need to catch up. Slow eats, good talk.
Wing King is a solid stop after coaching practice. Order the lemon pepper.
Marcus took Denise here for their date night. She's talked about it since.
Diner on Greenview is a working man's spot. Coffee comes hot and refills come fast.
Corner cafe has decent breakfast. Kitchen is a little slow on Saturday morning.
Bringing Jasmine here when she's in from Chicago is tradition now.
Motown-era prices, not really, but the food takes me back to when they were.
Bakery down on the corner has good sweet potato hand pies. Not overloaded on filling.
Ate here with the deacon board after a long meeting. Nobody complained.
Won the office lunch vote three weeks running for the plant.
Weekend line is long but they turn tables fast. Server was efficient and kind.
Brought Wolverines coaches here to plan the season. Kitchen kept the food coming.
That fish fry Friday special is the reason my belt got tighter this year.
Would visit again for the ambience alone. Food is a solid second reason.
Comfort food that tastes like Sunday dinner at Grandma's.
Kitchen ran out of the special early. Substitute was fine but not what I wanted.
Best neck bones I've had outside of Ma's kitchen. And that's saying something.
Family reunion planning session held here over pancakes. Everyone left smiling.
Denise says the collards taste right. That's the only opinion that matters.
Bit pricey for the portion but I'd come back for the fried okra alone.
Catfish Corner keeps a Detroit tradition alive.
Bert's Marketplace at Eastern Market on a Saturday is a whole vibe.
Motor City Soul Cafe has a solid Sunday plate. Music selection is on point.
Smokey G's smokehouse gets close to my rig but nothing beats Old Faithful.
Fishbone's in Greektown for a treat night with Denise. Cajun done right.
Denise's Sunday Table lives up to the name. Sweet tea refills without asking.
Norma G's roti roll took me back to a trip Denise and I never actually took to the islands.
Sweet Potato Sensations dessert samplers are dangerous, especially the praline.
Beans & Cornbread lunch after Bible study is a habit I can't shake.
Post-shift plate here after the chassis line grind. Hits every time.
Denise and I got the Sunday brunch, we couldn't stop talking about it all week.
Marcus loves the wings here. Perfect stop after a Wolverines game.
Jasmine flew in from Chicago and this was her first ask. Delivered.
Ruth's favorite peach cobbler in the city. She has standards.
Neighborhood gem. Reasonable prices, warm service.
Fried catfish flakes just right. Old school Detroit soul food.
Rib tips slathered right. Sauce leans sweet with a smoke chase.
Live piano on Sunday afternoons is a nice touch. Food is comforting.
Sunday after service, the whole deacon board landed here. Kitchen kept up.
The candied yams are a menu highlight. Just enough butter, brown sugar right.
Cornbread came out warm and crumbly. Good honey butter side.
Their sweet tea is dangerous. I drank three glasses.
Fried okra crispy, not greasy. Rare in this city.
Chicken and waffles the classic Detroit combo. Butter and syrup done right.
Coach Raymond and I broke down the season plan over grits and eggs here.
Pastor Caldwell recommended it. Now I recommend it. Trust the pastor.
Kids' menu covers the basics. Marcus was small once and it worked.
Family reunion planning session held over pancakes. Zero complaints.
Anniversary dinner for the 27th time. Denise glowed all night.
Wait can get long on Sundays. Come after 2 PM if you want a table quick.
Their Motown-era jukebox is the vibe I wish more spots kept.
Best sweet potato pie outside my house. And Denise doesn't disagree.
Ambience is old-school Detroit dining. I like that.
Neck bones done right. Slow cooked. Falls off. Like Ma's.
Salmon patties took me back to Grandma's kitchen. Well seasoned.
Fried chicken skin crackled proper. Meat juicy inside.
Waffle came out golden. Butter melted right in. Coffee refills fast.
Best hash browns on the East Side. Crispy edges, tender inside.
Corner cafe with breakfast until noon. Good for weekend brunch.
That collards side was exactly like Mama's. Vinegar and smoke.
Homestyle diner vibe. Blue collar friendly prices.
Sunday brunch after church is a Detroit tradition. This spot honors it.
The gumbo was a surprise standout. Rich, spicy, right.
Blackberry cobbler alternative when peach's out. Almost as good.
Wing sauce lineup is impressive. Lemon pepper is elite.
Comfort food that reminds me why Detroit still stands strong.
Brought a couple deacons here after a long meeting. Everyone left full.
Denise says the mac and cheese is the standard. That's the review.
Ronald B. and I stopped by after the barbershop. Sunday tradition.
